questions.stem:
—I ________ my homework. Can I watch TV now, Mom?
—Yes, but you must clean your room first. You ________ it last week.
questions.explanation: 第一空：强调过去的动作对现在造成的影响（作业已完成，所以现在可以看电视），用现在完成时have finished；第二空：last week是明确的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时didn't clean。现在完成时不能与具体的过去时间状语（如last week, yesterday）连用。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ________ is standing under the tree is my cousin. He is holding a book ________ is very interesting.
questions.explanation: 定语从句关系词选择：先行词the boy指人，在从句中作主语，用who；先行词a book指物，在从句中作主语，用which。口诀：人物who，物which，that人物物皆可替。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ________ to the school library tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 被动语态构成：主语books是动作return的承受者，需用被动语态；tomorrow morning提示用一般将来时，结构为will be+过去分词。一般将来时被动语态：will be done。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Do you know ________?
—I'm not sure. It ________ be sunny, but it depends on the weather report.
options: A. if will it rain; must
B. whether it will rain; might
C. that it will rain; can't
D. whether will it rain; must
questions.explanation: 第一空考查宾语从句语序，必须用陈述语序（主语+谓语），排除A和D；第二空根据I'm not sure可知表示不确定的推测，用might（可能）。must表肯定推测（一定是），can't表否定推测（不可能），均不符合语境。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The old man felt very ________ because he lived alone and had no friends to talk to.
questions.explanation: lonely表示'孤独的、寂寞的'，带有感情色彩；alone表示'独自的'，客观状态；happily（开心地）和luckily（幸运地）与语境不符。句中felt是系动词，后接形容词，且根据'lived alone and had no friends'可知表达情感上的孤独。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add the prefix 'un-' to the word 'comfortable', you get a new word which means ________.
questions.explanation: 前缀un-表示否定，意为'不、无'，comfortable（舒适的）+ un- = uncomfortable（不舒适的）。常见否定前缀：un-（unhappy），dis-（dislike），im-（impossible），in-（incorrect）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ________ a plan to protect the environment in our school.
questions.explanation: come up with意为'想出、提出（主意、计划等）'；look up to意为'尊敬'；run out of意为'用完、耗尽'；get along with意为'与……相处'。根据宾语'a plan'（一个计划），应选'想出计划'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The purpose was to encourage students to protect the environment. We were divided into several groups and given different tasks.
My group was responsible for (8)________ the rubbish in the school garden. At first, some students thought it was boring and dirty. However, as we worked together, we found it quite (9)________. We collected bottles, paper and other waste. To our surprise, we collected over 50 kilos of rubbish in just two hours!
After cleaning, we (10)________ the waste into different types. Bottles and paper were sent to the recycling center. Food waste was used to make compost for the school flowers. Through this activity, we not only made our campus cleaner, (11)________ learned the importance of waste sorting.
The most exciting part was the sharing session. Everyone talked about their feelings and experiences. I realized that environmental protection is not just a slogan, (12)________ something we should do in daily life. Small actions can make a big difference.
questions.explanation: 根据下文'We collected bottles...'可知此处指'收集垃圾'。collecting（收集）符合语境；making（制作）、producing（生产）、borrowing（借）均与'rubbish'搭配不当。解题技巧：完形填空要注意上下文线索，下文明确出现了collected的原词复现。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The purpose was to encourage students to protect the environment. We were divided into several groups and given different tasks.
My group was responsible for (8)________ the rubbish in the school garden. At first, some students thought it was boring and dirty. However, as we worked together, we found it quite (9)________. We collected bottles, paper and other waste. To our surprise, we collected over 50 kilos of rubbish in just two hours!
After cleaning, we (10)________ the waste into different types. Bottles and paper were sent to the recycling center. Food waste was used to make compost for the school flowers. Through this activity, we not only made our campus cleaner, (11)________ learned the importance of waste sorting.
The most exciting part was the sharing session. Everyone talked about their feelings and experiences. I realized that environmental protection is not just a slogan, (12)________ something we should do in daily life. Small actions can make a big difference.
questions.explanation: 前文提到'At first...boring and dirty'（起初认为无聊且脏），However表示转折，说明后来态度转变，应选与boring相反的词。interesting（有趣的）符合语境；difficult（困难的）、meaningless（无意义的）、terrible（糟糕的）均为负面词汇，与转折逻辑不符。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The purpose was to encourage students to protect the environment. We were divided into several groups and given different tasks.
My group was responsible for (8)________ the rubbish in the school garden. At first, some students thought it was boring and dirty. However, as we worked together, we found it quite (9)________. We collected bottles, paper and other waste. To our surprise, we collected over 50 kilos of rubbish in just two hours!
After cleaning, we (10)________ the waste into different types. Bottles and paper were sent to the recycling center. Food waste was used to make compost for the school flowers. Through this activity, we not only made our campus cleaner, (11)________ learned the importance of waste sorting.
The most exciting part was the sharing session. Everyone talked about their feelings and experiences. I realized that environmental protection is not just a slogan, (12)________ something we should do in daily life. Small actions can make a big difference.
questions.explanation: 固定搭配divide...into...意为'把……分成……'。后文提到'Bottles and paper were sent...Food waste was used...'说明进行了分类处理。cut into（切成）、put into（放入）、turn into（变成）均不符合'分类'的语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The purpose was to encourage students to protect the environment. We were divided into several groups and given different tasks.
My group was responsible for (8)________ the rubbish in the school garden. At first, some students thought it was boring and dirty. However, as we worked together, we found it quite (9)________. We collected bottles, paper and other waste. To our surprise, we collected over 50 kilos of rubbish in just two hours!
After cleaning, we (10)________ the waste into different types. Bottles and paper were sent to the recycling center. Food waste was used to make compost for the school flowers. Through this activity, we not only made our campus cleaner, (11)________ learned the importance of waste sorting.
The most exciting part was the sharing session. Everyone talked about their feelings and experiences. I realized that environmental protection is not just a slogan, (12)________ something we should do in daily life. Small actions can make a big difference.
questions.explanation: 固定搭配not only...but (also)...意为'不但……而且……'。句意：通过这次活动，我们不但使校园更干净，而且学到了垃圾分类的重要性。also可省略。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held an activity called 'Green Campus, Green Life'. The purpose was to encourage students to protect the environment. We were divided into several groups and given different tasks.
My group was responsible for (8)________ the rubbish in the school garden. At first, some students thought it was boring and dirty. However, as we worked together, we found it quite (9)________. We collected bottles, paper and other waste. To our surprise, we collected over 50 kilos of rubbish in just two hours!
After cleaning, we (10)________ the waste into different types. Bottles and paper were sent to the recycling center. Food waste was used to make compost for the school flowers. Through this activity, we not only made our campus cleaner, (11)________ learned the importance of waste sorting.
The most exciting part was the sharing session. Everyone talked about their feelings and experiences. I realized that environmental protection is not just a slogan, (12)________ something we should do in daily life. Small actions can make a big difference.
questions.explanation: 固定搭配not...but...意为'不是……而是……'。句意：我意识到环境保护不只是一个口号，而是我们在日常生活中应该做的事情。与前面的not just形成呼应。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Smart Homes: Life in the Future

Imagine waking up in the morning. The curtains open automatically, letting in the warm sunlight. The coffee machine starts working, filling the room with a pleasant smell. Meanwhile, the bathroom heater has already warmed your towel. This is not a scene from a science fiction movie, but a real smart home.

A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. It connects different home appliances (电器) to the internet. Residents can control these devices through their smartphones or voice commands. For example, you can turn on the air conditioner on your way home, so the room is cool when you arrive.

Besides convenience, smart homes also help save energy. Smart lights can adjust (调整) their brightness according to the natural light in the room. When no one is at home, the system automatically turns off unnecessary appliances. Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

However, some people worry about safety and privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the home network, they might control the devices or steal personal information. Experts suggest using strong passwords and updating software regularly to protect smart home systems.

As technology develops, smart homes are becoming more affordable (负担得起的). In the near future, more families will be able to enjoy this new way of living.
questions.stem: According to the passage, what can residents use to control smart home devices?
options: C. Special computers in the house.
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第二段第三句'Residents can control these devices through their smartphones or voice commands.'可知，居民可以通过智能手机或语音指令控制设备。A选项'只有遥控器'、C选项'屋内专用电脑'、D选项'只有自动机器人'均未在文中提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Smart Homes: Life in the Future

Imagine waking up in the morning. The curtains open automatically, letting in the warm sunlight. The coffee machine starts working, filling the room with a pleasant smell. Meanwhile, the bathroom heater has already warmed your towel. This is not a scene from a science fiction movie, but a real smart home.

A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. It connects different home appliances (电器) to the internet. Residents can control these devices through their smartphones or voice commands. For example, you can turn on the air conditioner on your way home, so the room is cool when you arrive.

Besides convenience, smart homes also help save energy. Smart lights can adjust (调整) their brightness according to the natural light in the room. When no one is at home, the system automatically turns off unnecessary appliances. Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

However, some people worry about safety and privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the home network, they might control the devices or steal personal information. Experts suggest using strong passwords and updating software regularly to protect smart home systems.

As technology develops, smart homes are becoming more affordable (负担得起的). In the near future, more families will be able to enjoy this new way of living.
questions.stem: What can we infer from the last paragraph?
options: A. Smart homes are too expensive for most families now.
B. Technology makes smart homes cheaper and more popular.
C. People don't like the new way of living in smart homes.
D. Smart homes will replace traditional homes completely.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。最后一段提到'As technology develops, smart homes are becoming more affordable.'（随着科技发展，智能家居正变得更负担得起），以及'more families will be able to enjoy this new way of living'（更多家庭将能享受这种新生活方式），可推断出科技使智能家居更便宜且更普及。A选项与原文'becoming more affordable'不符；C选项与原文'enjoy'矛盾；D选项'完全取代传统家居'过于绝对，文中未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Smart Homes: Life in the Future

Imagine waking up in the morning. The curtains open automatically, letting in the warm sunlight. The coffee machine starts working, filling the room with a pleasant smell. Meanwhile, the bathroom heater has already warmed your towel. This is not a scene from a science fiction movie, but a real smart home.

A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. It connects different home appliances (电器) to the internet. Residents can control these devices through their smartphones or voice commands. For example, you can turn on the air conditioner on your way home, so the room is cool when you arrive.

Besides convenience, smart homes also help save energy. Smart lights can adjust (调整) their brightness according to the natural light in the room. When no one is at home, the system automatically turns off unnecessary appliances. Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

However, some people worry about safety and privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the home network, they might control the devices or steal personal information. Experts suggest using strong passwords and updating software regularly to protect smart home systems.

As technology develops, smart homes are becoming more affordable (负担得起的). In the near future, more families will be able to enjoy this new way of living.
questions.stem: The underlined word 'adjust' in Paragraph 3 probably means ________.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文'according to the natural light in the room'（根据房间内的自然光）可知，智能灯应该能'调整'亮度。change（改变、调整）与adjust意思最接近；produce（生产）、check（检查）、repair（修理）均不符合语境。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Smart Homes: Life in the Future

Imagine waking up in the morning. The curtains open automatically, letting in the warm sunlight. The coffee machine starts working, filling the room with a pleasant smell. Meanwhile, the bathroom heater has already warmed your towel. This is not a scene from a science fiction movie, but a real smart home.

A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. It connects different home appliances (电器) to the internet. Residents can control these devices through their smartphones or voice commands. For example, you can turn on the air conditioner on your way home, so the room is cool when you arrive.

Besides convenience, smart homes also help save energy. Smart lights can adjust (调整) their brightness according to the natural light in the room. When no one is at home, the system automatically turns off unnecessary appliances. Studies show that smart homes can reduce energy use by up to 30%.

However, some people worry about safety and privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the home network, they might control the devices or steal personal information. Experts suggest using strong passwords and updating software regularly to protect smart home systems.

As technology develops, smart homes are becoming more affordable (负担得起的). In the near future, more families will be able to enjoy this new way of living.
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Home Appliances
B. How to Save Energy at Home
C. Smart Homes: A New Way of Living
D. The Dangers of Modern Technology
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章第一段介绍智能家居的场景，第二段讲其便利性，第三段讲节能环保，第四段讲安全问题，第五段展望未来。全文围绕'智能家居'这一主题展开，介绍这种新的生活方式。A选项'家电历史'偏离主题；B选项'如何在家节能'只是第三段内容；D选项'现代技术的危险'只是第四段内容，均过于片面。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Balancing study and hobbies is important for middle school students. Many students find it hard to manage their time well. (17)________, with some useful methods, you can do both well.

First, make a schedule. Write down your schoolwork and hobby time in a timetable. (18)________, you won't forget important tasks or miss your favorite activities. This helps you use time wisely.

(19)________, choose hobbies that can help with your study. For example, reading novels improves your language skills. Playing chess trains your thinking ability. In this way, hobbies become helpful tools for learning.

Finally, learn to say no to distractions (分心的事物). When doing homework, put away your phone. (20)________, find a quiet place to focus on your work. Remember, quality matters more than quantity.
questions.explanation: 前文提到'很多学生发现很难管理好时间'，后文说'有了一些有用的方法，你可以两者都做好'，前后是转折关系。However（然而）表示转折，符合逻辑。For example（例如）用于举例；First（首先）用于列举；Then（然后）表示顺承；In addition（此外）表示递进，均不符合此处转折语境。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Balancing study and hobbies is important for middle school students. Many students find it hard to manage their time well. (17)________, with some useful methods, you can do both well.

First, make a schedule. Write down your schoolwork and hobby time in a timetable. (18)________, you won't forget important tasks or miss your favorite activities. This helps you use time wisely.

(19)________, choose hobbies that can help with your study. For example, reading novels improves your language skills. Playing chess trains your thinking ability. In this way, hobbies become helpful tools for learning.

Finally, learn to say no to distractions (分心的事物). When doing homework, put away your phone. (20)________, find a quiet place to focus on your work. Remember, quality matters more than quantity.
questions.explanation: 前文说'在时间表中写下作业和爱好时间'，后文说'你不会忘记重要任务或错过喜欢的活动'，这是顺承关系，表示'这样做了之后'的结果。Then（然后、那样）符合逻辑，表示接下来的结果。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Balancing study and hobbies is important for middle school students. Many students find it hard to manage their time well. (17)________, with some useful methods, you can do both well.

First, make a schedule. Write down your schoolwork and hobby time in a timetable. (18)________, you won't forget important tasks or miss your favorite activities. This helps you use time wisely.

(19)________, choose hobbies that can help with your study. For example, reading novels improves your language skills. Playing chess trains your thinking ability. In this way, hobbies become helpful tools for learning.

Finally, learn to say no to distractions (分心的事物). When doing homework, put away your phone. (20)________, find a quiet place to focus on your work. Remember, quality matters more than quantity.
questions.explanation: 前文有First（首先），后文有Finally（最后），此处应是第二条建议。In addition（此外、另外）用于补充添加新的建议，符合列举逻辑。注意：虽然Second也可以，但选项中只有In addition符合递进补充关系。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Balancing study and hobbies is important for middle school students. Many students find it hard to manage their time well. (17)________, with some useful methods, you can do both well.

First, make a schedule. Write down your schoolwork and hobby time in a timetable. (18)________, you won't forget important tasks or miss your favorite activities. This helps you use time wisely.

(19)________, choose hobbies that can help with your study. For example, reading novels improves your language skills. Playing chess trains your thinking ability. In this way, hobbies become helpful tools for learning.

Finally, learn to say no to distractions (分心的事物). When doing homework, put away your phone. (20)________, find a quiet place to focus on your work. Remember, quality matters more than quantity.
questions.explanation: 前文说'做作业时把手机收起来'，后文说'找一个安静的地方专心学习'，这是并列补充关系，都是关于如何避免分心的具体做法。In addition（此外）表示在原有基础上补充另一个方法。However表转折，For example用于举例（但后文不是例子，而是另一个建议），First用于开头，Then侧重时间顺序，此处用In addition最恰当。
questions.label: 短文填空
